action.reasoning:
Observation: I need to check the model.py file to see the actual constructor signature of WeatherForecaster. From the output, I can see that the model constructor doesn't have an 'output_channels' parameter, but the predict.py script is trying to pass one. Looking at the model definition, I can see that the output channels are determined by the 'input_channels' parameter in the out_conv layer: 'self.out_conv = nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, input_channels, 1)'.

Analysis: The WeatherForecaster model takes 'input_channels' as a parameter which determines both the input and output channels. The model expects the output to have the same number of channels as the input. In our case, both input and output should have 2 channels (Z500 and T850). The predict.py script should instantiate the model with input_channels=2, hidden_channels=80, but without the 'output_channels' parameter.

Decision: I'll update the predict.py script to remove the 'output_channels' parameter and just use 'input_channels=2' which will automatically set the output channels to 2 as well.
